title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: A dog named Chevy needs a brace; a test shows how much pressure dogs can put on their legs after receiving braces or prosthetics.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Anchorage, Alaska, is home to hundreds of black bears; it's these black bears, that are patrolling the city; with their brains, their brawn and their cunning, these bears have mastered life in the urban jungle.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Anchorage has a resident who is renowned as nature's lumberjack and North America's largest rodent, the beaver; with their powerful teeth, the trees they fell can be huge and their dams can be seen from space.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Anchorage is home to a huge number of wild porcupines, living in the city's parks, gardens and streets; almost twice the average size, Anchorage's porcupines search for food in the city and find refuge during Alaskan winters.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Travelling 3500 miles through rivers and streams, salmon are Alaska's hardiest resident; there is one hazard they face right at the end of their journey; it's the brown bears.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: The Dog Dudes are surprised when they get a call from a widower about an uncontrollable Labrador wreaking havoc in her life. Usually a tame breed, Hugo became a big problem when his human leader passed away unexpectedly three years ago.

title: Super Senses: Special Forces
subTitle: The mantis shrimp's visual systems; the owl's hearing; the extensive sensorimotor specializations of the elephant's trunk.

title: Special Forces
subTitle: In the water, it takes sophisticated navigation systems, fine-tuned reflexes, and high-precision detection methods to survive. These animals have what it takes.

title: Super Senses: Special Forces
subTitle: Sight, hearing, smell, touch, taste and beyond; survival demands an edge; these animals' abilities qualify them for nature's special forces.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: Binita steals a policeman's radio; a truck is carrying an unexpected stowaway.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: Zamir tries to find his family; Kamal proves he is worthy; Teejay searches for his mother.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: Kamal leads a hospital raid; Teejay fights for his life; Zamir finds his old troop.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: Annual elephant festival in Jaipur; Kamal is captured; a face from the past.

title: The Secret Life Of Baby Owls
subTitle: Little owls Geronimo and Flo are still reeling from their recent tragedy.

title: Inside Animal A&E
subTitle: Ambulance drivers Sonia and Angela pick up a 50-kilo Dogue De Bordeaux who has a mysterious bed-wetting problem, which Cristina must try to understand; Lawrence is trying to find out why guinea pig Noel is feeling anxious and upset.

title: Inside Animal A&E
subTitle: Behind the scenes at the Blue Cross animal hospitals in London Victoria and Grimsby.

title: Inside Animal A&E
subTitle: A 17-year-old cat, a bulldog with a mystery swelling, newborn pups, and a mischievous puppy test the Blue Cross team's skill and compassion.

title: Inside Animal A&E
subTitle: The team springs into action for a puppy hit by a car, Westie's unusual surgery and a Staffordshire that fears fireworks.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: A look at practices for seeing great white sharks up close, including baiting or chumming, which are being questioned and banned in many countries due to a dramatic increase in shark attacks.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: Seals and sea lions are observed in their natural habitat.

title: Sumatra's Last Tigers
subTitle: Tiger populations throughout the world are crashing due to poaching and deforestation; Tomy Winata runs the Tambling Wildlife Nature Conservation, a safe haven for tigers where they may undergo rehabilitation.

title: The Secret Life of the Circlers
subTitle: Exploring the Black Harriers journey from egg to fledgling.

title: The Best of Worlds: Life at Its Alpine Limits
subTitle: The Alps are home to plants and animals that owe their success to an amazing capacity to live in conditions that for other species, would be barely tolerable. For them however, this is everyday life.

subTitle: Discovering creatures that have evolved to incorporate spitting and stinging into their deadly arsenal for surviving in the wild.

subTitle: For these creatures, ejection and odor are potent instruments to capture prey, as well as a means of survival.

subTitle: Marine naturalist and underwater filmmaker Jonathan Bird discusses animal themes, marine research, underwater exploration and recent discoveries.

title: Kalahari Meerkats
subTitle: Cleo, the leader of the Gosa gang, gives birth to five pups, and the rest of the gang is excited to welcome the new members to the family.

title: Animal Armoury
subTitle: When wielded precisely, teeth and claws are instruments for hunting, defense and combat in the animal world.

title: Animal Armoury
subTitle: The electric catfish and electric ray use shocks to attack their victims, while elephants and kangaroos fight with their feet.

title: Animal Armoury
subTitle: Animals like the Arctic fox, green tree frog and penguin, benefit from colouring that matches or blends with their surroundings.

title: Animal Armoury
subTitle: Showcases how some animals and creatures, such as toads, moths and stone fish, use their poison glands to survive in the wild.

subTitle: The incredible images of the Guizhou Golden Monkey population, captured by China's scientific research station in the Fanjing Mountains and taken over a few years using infrared cameras.

title: Green Tree Ants: Friend or Foe?
subTitle: An investigation of green tree ants' super-sized satellite outposts, their rise to power, wars and submissions.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Showcases some of the most venomous creatures in the animal kingdom and highlights the different ways they hunt prey.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Takes a look at the hierarchy of animals in the wild as they fight each other to survive and try to emerge as the apex predator.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: The filmmakers shed light on how the Nile crocodile, the Gaboon viper and the great white shark survive using their fangs and teeth as weapons.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Takes a look at the heavyweights of the animal kingdom, like the Goliath grouper, and examines how they survive in the wild.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Takes a look at how dangerous animals like the hyena and killer whale use their sharp minds to survive in the wild.

title: Python Hunters
subTitle: The hunters catch invasive Burmese Pythons to determine how the predator became established in the Everglades.

title: Python Hunters
subTitle: The Hunters investigate how the Burmese Pythons have survived the worst cold snap in South Florida in decades.

title: Python Hunters
subTitle: Sean, Greg and Mike head to the edge of a sawgrass marsh in search of pythons.

title: Chameleons of the World
subTitle: A real life look at how chameleons reproduce and hide from predators.

title: Lions and Giants
subTitle: Usually elephants are too large and dangerous for predators to attack. But in one place in Africa they are falling prey to a mystery killer.

title: The Last Lion of Liuwa
subTitle: The last remaining lioness on the plain is the sole survivor of a massacre that took the rest of the pride.

subTitle: A spider monkey named Infinity was stolen from the forest as a baby; now, after an eight-year rehabilitation, she is ready to return to the wild.

subTitle: Whale sharks in Mexico; shark hunting ritual off the coast of Costa Rica.

subTitle: Angel sharks in California's Channel Islands and reef sharks in Mexico.

subTitle: Tiger sharks; night diving with sharks; how sharks smell.

subTitle: Tiger sharks; night diving with sharks; how sharks smell.

subTitle: In a corner of rural Essex, a former First World War airfield has been left wild for over 100 years.

title: The Secret Life Of Baby Owls
subTitle: Little owls Geronimo and Flo are still reeling from their recent tragedy.

title: Wild and Alive
subTitle: Five critically endangered African species find protection in a modest haven, De Hoop Nature Reserve; it is not only a World Heritage Site but also a necessity for the survival of these animals.

title: Wild and Alive
subTitle: On the coast of South Africa, a group of mammoth mammals arrives from the deep ocean; every year, humpback and southern right whales make migrations from the Antarctic seas to mate and give birth.

title: Wild and Alive
subTitle: In a small rural village in the heart of the Ivory Coast, lives a special resident; the last white rhino in the country sleeps on the village outskirts and feeds on the villagers' crops.

title: Wild and Alive
subTitle: On the savannah of southern Africa, rains have turned the land green and lush; it's the time of plenty and for the cast of wild characters cash in.

title: Python Hunters
subTitle: The guys return to the Everglades to see if Florida's bitter winter put a dent in the Burmese python population.

title: Python Hunters
subTitle: The team hunts the elusive Nile monitor lizard.

title: Python Hunters
subTitle: The team heads to Louisiana to conduct research on invasive reptile species.

title: Python Hunters
subTitle: The guys attempt to track down a massive breeding snake in hopes of putting a dent in the invasive snake population.

title: Animal Science
subTitle: An animal series with a uniquely scientific approach shows how and why creatures are able to excel.

title: Paseka - The Easter Elephant
subTitle: On Easter morning, a herd mysteriously abandons a baby elephant in the wilds of Botswana's Okavango Delta, but she survives and joins an unusual captive herd.

title: Wild Animal Rescue
subTitle: A medical emergency arises when six tigers are flown from the Netherlands to South Africa; treating lions with medical problems proves to be a challenge.

title: Wild Animal Rescue
subTitle: A dangerous mission into the Gaza Strip has Four Paws dealing with tricky negotiations and red tape in an attempt to rescue two lion cubs from their owner.

title: Wild Animal Rescue
subTitle: Four Paws rescues 15 Kosovo bears from their small cages in private zoos and restaurants; a team goes to Romania's Danube Delta to rescue Europe's last wild horses from the slaughterhouse.

title: Wild Animal Rescue
subTitle: Four Paws gives a new home to a family of five lions who have suffered a harsh life in a Romanian zoo; the team talks about a legal industry that threatens South Africa's lions.

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: Classified as critically endangered, Black Rhinos are known for their unpredictable and aggressive behavior when approached or caught unaware; join the capture team in a capture exercise at the Hluhluwe-Imfolozi Park.

subTitle: Marine naturalist and underwater filmmaker Jonathan Bird discusses animal themes, marine research, underwater exploration and recent discoveries.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Deep water enthusiast Jonathan Bird passes a scuba diving test with Zac Petersen.

title: Wild Iberia
subTitle: The time of stillness and silence has come, but most animals aren't able to hibernate; many species have had to adapt to this continental way of life.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: The arctic city is home to over 2000 moose; the notorious animals are a running riot; crossing busy highways, giving birth in backyards and battling in suburbia.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Anchorage, ice-bound nearly all the year, explodes with life in summer; Northern flora and fauna adapt to the brief season, accelerating their activities to maximize the short window of opportunity.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Alaska stands as Earth's last true wilderness; Its 600 square miles of pristine habitat host diverse wildlife; Caribou herds roam plains, wolves stalk forests, and brown bears rule rivers in this abundant ecosystem.

subTitle: On the savannah, in the forest or under the sea, the competition never quits; having the right gear provides more than a fighting chance for success.
